raw_consumer_statement:
I write to follow up on Complaint No. XXXX of XX/XX/XXXX which remains without a written response from Barclays, and now it appears Barclays has taken action which puts my health insurance coverage at risk. I have repeatedly called the direct line included in Barclays letter of XX/XX/XXXX and have been unable to speak to anyone despite the now urgency of the matter. 

The issue, first reported on XXXX XXXX, XXXX, is an overcharge in the amount {$280.00}. Barclays has gone back and forth between temporarily reversing such amount and then reapplying it to the account. Since my last complaint of XX/XX/XXXX, it appears that Barclays contacted the merchant, cancelled its original XX/XX/XXXX payment to the merchant, and then paid the merchant an amount {$280.00} less than the original payment amount all of this without my knowledge, consent, nor any communication with me. Now, the merchant is contacting me for payment of the {$280.00}. Thus, I am still being overcharged in the amount of {$280.00}, since I paid Barclays an amount of {$280.00} above the amount which Barclays paid the merchant. The merchant happens to be my health insurance provider. Thus, Barclays now has put my health insurance coverage at risk. 

When Barclays explained in its XX/XX/XXXX letter referenced-above that its contract stipulated a XXXX  % foreign transaction fee and provided me with a copy of such contract, I proceeded to pay the approximately {$400.00} fee which I had originally questioned even though I found it very excessive. I mention this, because ( i ) it was a second issue included in an earlier complaint, and ( ii ) as example of an issue that was resolved once Barclays provided information in response to my complaint. 

Since XX/XX/XXXX, Barclays has not yet been able to provide an explanation of the apparent overcharge. I simply do not understand how I charge one amount on my account and Barclays charges me {$280.00} more. Nor do I understand how in its effort to handle this matter, Barclays now reverses the XX/XX/XXXX transaction with the merchant to now pay it {$280.00} less than the original payment, while Barclays received a higher amount from me. If there is an explanation for this, I ask that Barclays provide it. If there is no explanation, then Barclays should correct the overcharge. 

Lastly, I reiterate how extremely difficult it is to have any communication with or response from Barclays. Since XX/XX/XXXX, I have been attempting to resolve this issue. I am very interested in resolving it once and for all and closing my account with Barclays as soon as possible. 

Thank you.
